channel.title: CI/CD on Dennys Diary
channel.description: Recent content in CI/CD on Dennys Diary
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2008–2018, Steve Francia and the Hugo Authors; all rights reserved.

item.title: Top SonarQube Plugins for Enhanced Code Quality
item.description:
Dependency-Check Plugin for SonarQube

If you use DependencyCheck to detect publicly disclosed vulnerabilities in your project's dependencies, and you want to integrate the result into SonarQube, you can use this plugin to do it.

Use command line

  1. You can execute this command to generate dependency-check-report.json and dependency-check-report.html. (If you use Python, please add --enableExperimental parameter)

    1dependency-check.sh -n --scan "." --format ALL --project "$PROJECT_NAME" --failOnCVSS 0 --enableExperimental

  2. You can add the 2 parameters when you call SonarQube, and it will import the reports generated by Dependency-Check.

item.title: Run SonarQube on Linux Docker with Mono to scan .NET 4.8 Code
item.description:
Requirement

We know how to run SonarQube to scan .NET 4 code in GitLab + SonarQube + .NET 4.8 (non-docker), but it needs a Windows environment and does not support Linux.

Therefore, we want to find a solution to run SonarQube on Linux in docker if possible. In this artiflce, we try to use Mono framework (a cross platform .NET 4.7 framework) to run SonarQube on Linux to scan .NET 4 code.

item.title: GitLab SonarQube Integration with .NET
item.description:
If you want to use SonarQube to scan your code during GitLab's CI/CD flow, please see the following procedures.

GitLab + SonarQube + .NET Core or .NET 5/6/7/... (docker)

  1. Refer to this document to integrate GitLab and SonarQube.

  2. Set the environment variable in GitLab

    • $SONAR_URL: The URL of SonarQube
    • $SONAR_TOKEN: The token to access SonarQube
    • $CI_PROJECT_DIR: This is a predifined variable in GitLab, you can reference https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/ci/variables/predefined_variables.html
    • $CI_JOB_NAME: This is a predifined variable in GitLab, you can reference https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/ci/variables/predefined_variables.html

  3. Add the following to your .gitlab-ci.yml

item.title: Use Grafana to Manage SonarQube KPI
item.description:
Requirement

Usually we use SonarQube to check the KPI of projects:

You can also check the build history of a project:

Use Grafana to Manager SonarQube KPI

But there are some SonarQube exporters, they can export SonarQube's KPI to Grafana/Prometheus.

Solution 1

  1. Exporter: https://github.com/dmeiners88/sonarqube-prometheus-exporter: It's a SonarQube plugin, but it only supports 5 KPIs, and the last commit date is 2018/11/9. It supports SonarQube 7, not sure if it supports 8 or 9, I don't plan to try it.

Solution 2

  1. Exporter: https://github.com/nthienan/sonarqube-exporter

item.title: Use Grafana to Manage GitLab CI/CD Pipelines
item.description:
Change log

  1. 2022/10/13: Add some check points.

Requirement

When there are lots of projects on GitLab, it's not easy to know the overall build status of their CI/CD Pipelines.

Use Grafana to Manage GitLab CI/CD Pipelines

Solution 1: Use GitLab build-in metrics

  1. Exporter: GitLab build-in (reference: https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/administration/monitoring/prometheus/)
  2. Grafana dashboard: https://grafana.com/grafana/dashboards/10620
    1. This dashboard needs this plugin: https://grafana.com/grafana/plugins/grafana-polystat-panel/
  3. prometheus.yml 
    1- job_name: 'GitLab'
2    scrape_interval: 5s
3    metrics_path: '/-/metrics'
4    scheme: https
5    static_configs:
6    - targets: ['gitlab.xxx.xxx.xxx']
7    params:
8    token: ['***************']

Solution 2: Use gitlab-ci-pipelines-exporter

  • Exporter: https://github.com/mvisonneau/gitlab-ci-pipelines-exporter This exporter provides several spectives metrics (with different Grafana dashboards)

  1. Grafana dashboard: the same as solution 1 (it also needs Ploystat plugin)

item.title: Use GitLab to do .NET 4.8 CI/CD
item.description:
Remind
This is for .NET 4.8 or below only!!

Precondition

You already have a GitLab (GitLab Saas or GitLab self-managed). This document is for .NET 4.X, if you want to build .NET core or .NET 5/6/7/..., you can use docker to run GitLab Runner.

Procedure

Step 1 - Prepare .NET build environment

  1. First, you need to prepare an environment to build .NET application. The easiest solution is to install Visual Studio. (Please let me know if I can build a .NET build environment without Visual Studio, thanks.)
  2. But it's strange, MSBuild.exe is not included in PATH after the installation. Please put C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\MSBuild\Current\Bin into your PATH.

Step 2 - Install Git client

Download and install Git for Windows. The portable version is ok, but because Git Runner is a Windows service, please add git.exe into PATH.
